assayPolicies.scope: reporting and ancestry or replication checks

outstandingRequirements.text: Confirm repository accession prerequisites and metadata package completeness.

acceptedReadRepositories.description: Repositories the pipeline is permitted to read FROM for raw data acquisition (distinct from depositionTargets, which governs publishing). Agents must NOT drop SME-named accessions solely because a tool only speaks GEO — route by accession prefix to the correct reader.

repositories.name: China National GeneBank — CNSA (CNGB Sequence Archive)
repositories.notes: CNGB CNSA hosts a substantial fraction of Chinese-authored scRNA-seq IVD and cancer atlases. Always included in the candidate pool for human-tissue scRNA-seq projects — dropping a CNGB-only study reduces cohort coverage without governance justification.

repositories.name: National Genomics Data Center (China) — Genome Sequence Archive
repositories.notes: NGDC GSA public-access partition. Use HRA (Human Restricted Access) only with DUA; the CRA + open GSA partitions are unrestricted.

repositories.name: European Genome-phenome Archive
repositories.notes: EGA is controlled-access by default — only read from EGA when explicit DUA is in the package manifest. Out of scope for public-data-only runs.

acceptedReadRepositories.acquisitionRoutingRule: When the SME-named cohort includes accessions from multiple repositories, the data_acquisition candidate method MUST dispatch per-accession-prefix and MUST NOT silently truncate the cohort. Dropping a study because the selected tool can't reach its repository is never a terminal action — surface a structured decision point with options {wait_for_repo_client | defer_to_later_pass | pull_via_alternative_repo_helper}.
